title: Rosebud Launch Control
objective: Guide as many rosebud micro-rockets to clean liftoff as possible before three bad calls end the run.

controls.input: Tap an opened bud with a glowing go-ring
controls.outcome: The bud fires, scores points, and builds combo

controls.input: Do not tap closed buds, red abort buds, or spent husks
controls.outcome: You avoid strikes and preserve your run

controls.input: Watch the large beacon color and warning chime every few seconds
controls.outcome: Only buds matching the current launch signal are valid

steps: Rosebud rockets rise up in a crowded vertical garden of launch rails.
A bud becomes tappable only after its petals peel open and a bright ring pulses around it.
Tapping a valid bud launches it upward and increases your combo multiplier.
Tapping a closed bud, a red-abort bud, a spent husk, or a bud with the wrong signal color gives one strike.
Every few seconds the launch tower announces a new signal state such as Gold Launch or Blue Launch, instantly changing which open buds are
Speed increases over time: more rails fill, fake-outs appear faster, and signal swaps happen with less warning.
The run ends after three strikes or when the 3-minute launch window expires.
scoring: Valid launch: 10 points. Consecutive valid launches raise a multiplier up to x5. Perfectly timing a tap on the ring pulse grants a small bonus. Final score feeds a simple local leaderboard for best single run and daily-c
tips: Tap late, not early; the open-petal ring is the real confirmation.
Use the tower beacon first and the bud color second after each signal swap.
Red siren plus descending tone always means abort, even if the bud is open.
Protect a high combo by skipping crowded edge cases when the board gets messy.

trendContext.title: Launch HN: Rosebud (YC S19) – Turn game descriptions into browser games
